task_completion.description: Modify Dockerfiles or entrypoint scripts to fix the startup issues.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open text editor (like VS Code, nano, or vim) or terminal output (like `cat` or `git diff`) showing edits to Dockerfiles or entrypoint scripts within the `/home/ga/entrypoint-debug/` directory.

task_completion.description: All three Docker Compose services (Gateway, API, Worker) are running without crashing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output of a command like `docker compose ps` or `docker ps` showing the Gateway, API, and Worker containers in an 'Up' or running state.

task_completion.description: The Gateway service responds successfully at http://localhost:8080.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output of `curl http://localhost:8080` (or `curl -I http://localhost:8080`) showing a successful HTTP response, or a web browser open to `http://localhost:8080` displaying the Gateway.

task_completion.description: The API service responds successfully at http://localhost:5000/health.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output of `curl http://localhost:5000/health` showing a successful response (e.g., JSON indicating health) or a web browser open to that URL.

task_completion.description: The Worker container processes jobs with the configured 5-second interval.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output of the Worker container logs (e.g., `docker compose logs worker`) showing repeated log entries indicating job processing, with timestamps approximately 5 seconds apart.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely interacted with Docker to run the stack.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the terminal shows actual Docker commands being executed (e.g., `docker compose up`, `docker compose build`) and standard Docker daemon build/run output, rather than just `echo` commands printing fake statuses.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely tested the endpoints using curl or a browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the terminal shows actual `curl` commands being executed with real HTTP response headers/bodies, or a real browser window is used, not just text files being printed to the console.

integrity.description: The agent modified the correct files in the specified directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the files being edited are explicitly located in `/home/ga/entrypoint-debug/` (visible in the terminal prompt, editor title bar, or file tree), rather than mock files created elsewhere.
